title: hibbertia prostrata
intro: Hibbertia prostrata, also known as the Guinea flower, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to eastern Australia. It has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to have health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating respiratory problems. Hibbertia prostrata can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infused oils, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
